opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: He's been screaming and pulling at his left ear for about three hours and nothing I do is settling him. He's got a bit of a temperature too and he had a runny nose and cough a few days ago. I've tried a bit of Panadol but it hasn't really helped.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The pain came on suddenly this evening, he's inconsolable and won't sleep, and I don't know what to do.

symptom_narrative.onset: the ear pain came on suddenly about three hours ago; the cold started three days ago
symptom_narrative.duration: ear pain a few hours, worsening over the last 12 hours; runny nose and cough for three days
symptom_narrative.character: he keeps grabbing and rubbing his left ear and crying like it really hurts
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: really bad — he's inconsolable and screaming, worst I've seen him
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant crying, worse when he lies down to sleep
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: being held and cuddled settles him a little
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: lying down flat seems to make the crying worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: he's warm, had a runny nose and dry cough for a few days, and doesn't seem to be hearing me as well on that side
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he's had a couple of colds but never an ear thing like this before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he won't sleep and can't be settled; the whole house is up

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: gave him a little bit about four hours ago

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: young child at home with parents; recent cold going around the house

objective_data_offered.value: about 38.1 degrees under the arm with the home thermometer
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: axillary reading on a home digital thermometer, single measurement in a distressed child

objective_data_offered.value: he's about 14 kg at his last weigh-in
his face looks flushed and he keeps tugging and rubbing the left ear; he's distressed but settles a bit when I hold him
he doesn't seem to turn to soft sounds on the left side like he normally does
he's still drinking and had a wet nappy earlier; no rash, and his neck seems to move normally
